THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CROSS DEFENDANT PACIFIC GULF MARINE, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PRESENTED ANY EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANT'S ACTIVITIES ON BOARD DEFENDANT'S SHIP CONTRIBUTED TO ANY INJURY. MR. WENZEL'S INTERPOSED MOTION FOR A CONTINUANCE OF HEARING WAS DENIED. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses, To Interrogatories And Requests For Production , That Facts Be Deemed Admitted, And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-26-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff Henry Paquin's Motion To Compel Responses To Interrogatories And Requests For Production, And That Facts Be Deemed Admitted is granted. No opposition has been filed. Defendant U.S. Ship Management, Inc. is ordered to respond to Plaintiff's Form and Special Interrogatories, Sets One and Two, and Plaintiff's Request For Production, Sets One and Two, within 15 days of notice of this order. Plaintiff's Requests For Admissions, Sets One and Two, are deemed admitted. Defendant U.S. Ship Management, Inc.'s failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant U.S. Ship Management, Inc. in the amount of $940, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
